entities.name: itikartavyatā
entities.cite: know-how, the whole practical intelligence by which life is conducted

entities.name: saṃskāra
entities.cite: the child comprehends through residues laid down before — in prior lives, on the tradition's reckoning; in any case, antecedently to this learning

axioms.name: pratipadyate
axioms.cite: the child comprehends through residues laid down before

denials.name: anindriya
denials.cite: Infant know-how is not word-free cognition but the operation of word-formed traces

claims.name: pratipadyate
claims.cite: the child comprehends, through the residues deposited in it before

rejected_readings.label: word_free_infant
rejected_readings.rendering: The child's purposive behavior before language-acquisition demonstrates knowledge independent of word.
rejected_readings.why: Treats pre-linguistic infant competence as a counterexample to word-dependence, ignoring that Bhartrhari dissolves this by grounding infant know-how in samskara—word-formed traces operating below conscious speech. This reading denies the sabdamula predication, cutting the explanatory link.

rejected_readings.label: empirical_nativism
rejected_readings.rendering: The child carries innate procedural capacities that are not themselves linguistic in nature.
rejected_readings.why: Acknowledges the empirical fact (pre-linguistic competence) but rejects the commentary's resolution: that samskara are word-formed. This reduces samskara to mere neurological residue, stripping the sabdamula predication and leaving the axiom unsupported.

notes: The verse defends universal word-dependence against empirical objection: infant competence is not a word-free counterexample but an operation of word-formed traces (samskara) that are themselves linguistic. Formalization encodes itikartavyata as a power dependent on sabda, with samskara as the manifestation through which this dependence persists beneath audible speech. Strain: the commentary invokes prior-life residues, which sits uneasily in a secular reading; the contract treats samskara as an abstract manifestation to bracket metaphysical commitment while preserving the ontological structure.
